fixture_note: FIXTURE — synthetic shape modeled on StableCrypto market-data x402 responses. NO real treasury balances. Used by N3 sim AND as the placeholder for the N4 paysh_live recorded fixture (overwritten when operator runs pytest -m live_paysh).

headers.X-Payment-Required: x402; network=solana-mainnet; asset=usdc; amount=0.01; recipient=<provider_pubkey>; nonce=<server_nonce>

body.narrative: StableCrypto returned a unified treasury and market-data view aggregating CoinGecko prices, DefiLlama TVL/yield, Alchemy chain telemetry, and Etherscan transfer history under a single x402-priced call. For neobank treasury operations the most relevant fields are: spot price for the held basket (USDC, USDT, DAI), 24h DEX pool depth on the venues the neobank routes through, and stablecoin issuer treasury holdings as a proxy for redemption-risk. The reference call returned a 142-element series across the 14 stablecoin pairs the neobank tracks; the recommended treasury cadence is one priced call per minute per pair during US business hours, dropping to per-five-minutes overnight, which keeps total per-day spend below 30 USDC for a 14-pair basket while still tightening the risk-window on a depeg event to a 60-second worst case. Edge cases observed: Alchemy gas estimates are stale by ~15s during chain congestion which matters for any auto-rebalance routine that picks gas budgets dynamically; prefer Etherscan's gas oracle as a tie-breaker. The endpoint cost is fixed at 0.01 USDC regardless of payload size which makes it materially cheaper than Tavily's text-based equivalents for structured numeric series. For Solana-native neobanks specifically, the on-chain DEX-pool slice covers Raydium, Orca, and Meteora — sufficient for the top-of-book pricing question; for L2 routing decisions you'd still need a separate provider.
